title: Quantitative Validation Against a Story Corpus
description: Tests archetype and genre graphs against a corpus of 50 well-known works drawn from the example works registry. For each work, scores archetype coverage (% of nodes clearly present), genre coverage (% of constraints satisfied), identifies missing patterns (graph elements with no story correspondent) and extra patterns (story elements not captured by the graph).

_methodology.selection_criteria: Selected from the 107-work registry to ensure coverage of all 15 archetypes and 27 genres, favoring works with clear structural alignment and cultural significance.

scoring.archetype_coverage: Percentage of the archetype's main-spine nodes that are clearly identifiable in the work. Variant nodes scored separately.
scoring.genre_coverage: Percentage of the genre's nodes (all levels) that are satisfied by the work. Severity-weighted: hard constraint misses are flagged.
scoring.confidence: high (unambiguous mapping), medium (defensible but debatable), low (stretch mapping)

extra_patterns: Ensemble dynamics not captured by single-protagonist Hero's Journey graph
corpus.notes: Textbook Hero's Journey. All 12 nodes map precisely. The Force is insufficiently grounded in SF extrapolation (more fantasy), hence the missed soft constraint.

extra_patterns: Red's parallel redemption arc not captured by single-protagonist Escape graph
corpus.notes: Near-perfect Escape archetype mapping. The long time horizon (20 years) inverts the typical escape urgency, making the time-pressure drama constraint inapplicable.

extra_patterns: Lady Macbeth's parallel arc of ambition and disintegration
corpus.notes: Textbook Tragedy. All 8 nodes map precisely. The supernatural elements (witches, ghost) extend beyond pure drama.

extra_patterns: Multiple parallel romance subplots (Jane/Bingley, Lydia/Wickham)
corpus.notes: Perfect comedy archetype. The romance genre constraints are nearly fully satisfied; forced proximity is soft and debatable.

extra_patterns: Multiple parallel quest threads (Ring-bearer, Rohan, Gondor) not captured by single-spine quest graph
corpus.notes: Definitive Quest archetype. All 9 nodes map precisely. The boundary between magical and mundane is porous rather than sharp.

extra_patterns: Nick as narrator-observer creates a frame structure not in the Rise and Fall graph
corpus.notes: Gatsby's fall is sudden (death) rather than a sustained decline, making the Fall node mapping debatable. The narration through Nick's eyes adds a layer the graph doesn't capture.

extra_patterns: Survivor-horror elimination structure (characters die one by one) not in the monster archetype
corpus.notes: Strong monster-horror blend. World is NOT fully restored (most crew is dead) — the resolution is survival, not restoration. The sequential elimination pattern is a horror-specific structure.

extra_patterns: Nemo's parallel coming-of-age arc in the fish tank
corpus.notes: Perfect quest mapping. Marlin's journey to find Nemo hits every quest node. The parallel Nemo subplot adds a coming-of-age layer the quest graph doesn't capture.

extra_patterns: Social horror/racial allegory not captured by either graph
Mystery-investigation element (discovering the truth) overlaps with Mystery Unveiled
corpus.notes: The escape archetype maps well but the setbacks phase is compressed — the revelation and escape happen rapidly. The social horror dimension is a thematic layer beyond both graphs.
Textbook rebirth AND textbook holiday. The three ghosts map perfectly to Warning → Resistance → Ordeal of Truth. Near-complete genre coverage.

extra_patterns: Non-linear narrative structure (told through flashbacks/newsreel) adds a mystery-investigation frame
corpus.notes: Perfect rise-and-fall mapping. The non-linear structure is a narrative technique not captured by the linear archetype graph.

extra_patterns: Quest structure overlaid on voyage-and-return (journey to Emerald City)
corpus.notes: Perfect voyage-and-return. Magic costs are minimal (ruby slippers work for free), partially violating the hard constraint.

extra_patterns: Extended philosophical deliberation (To be or not to be) not captured by revenge graph's action orientation
Play-within-a-play as investigation technique
corpus.notes: The revenge archetype maps well but Hamlet's famous inaction means the Pursuit phase is inverted — he delays rather than pursues. The philosophical depth exceeds the graph's structure.

extra_patterns: Political/institutional resistance (mayor refuses to close beach) adds a social dimension
corpus.notes: Perfect Overcoming the Monster mapping. The institutional resistance adds political drama not captured by the monster archetype.

extra_patterns: Body horror elements (physical transformation) extend beyond psychological genre
corpus.notes: Perfect transformation mapping. Nina's transformation from White Swan to Black Swan follows every node precisely.

extra_patterns: Rags-to-riches subplot (prison to wealth) overlaid on revenge structure
corpus.notes: Perfect revenge mapping. The adventure genre is a secondary classification — the story's adventure elements serve the revenge structure.

extra_patterns: Time-loop structure creates unique trial repetition not in any archetype graph
corpus.notes: Textbook rebirth within comedy. The time-loop is a brilliant structural device that the existing graphs cannot represent — each loop is a complete trial cycle.

extra_patterns: Courtroom drama provides a structural spine the coming-of-age graph doesn't capture
Social justice theme as thematic overlay
corpus.notes: Textbook coming-of-age through Scout's eyes. Atticus serves as the perfect mentor. The courtroom drama adds a genre layer beyond the archetype.

extra_patterns: Confined-space structure (Nakatomi Plaza) adds a thriller element
corpus.notes: Strong monster archetype in action genre. McClane has no preparation phase — he's thrown directly into confrontation, which is part of the film's appeal.

extra_patterns: Episodic trial structure (each island is a self-contained trial)
Penelope's parallel waiting arc
corpus.notes: Foundational voyage-and-return. The episodic island-hopping creates a trial structure the archetype graph represents but whose repetitive nature isn't fully captured.

extra_patterns: Dual-narrator structure (Nick and Amy) creates dramatic irony not in the mystery graph
The mystery's 'solution' doesn't bring understanding — it brings horror
corpus.notes: Strong mystery-thriller hybrid. The 'New Understanding' node is subverted — the truth is revealed but doesn't bring closure or wisdom, only trapped awareness.
Textbook rags-to-riches. The fairy godmother's magic has a time-based cost (midnight) but not the deeper narrative cost the fantasy constraint expects.

extra_patterns: Transformation archetype runs parallel — Walter White's identity transformation
corpus.notes: Perfect rise-and-fall in crime genre. Also maps to Tragedy. The dual archetype reading (rise-and-fall + transformation) demonstrates the value of hybrid archetype modeling.

extra_patterns: Philosophical/religious allegory layer not captured by either graph
corpus.notes: Textbook Hero's Journey in SF setting. The SF constraint about resolution is met in spirit (new equilibrium with Neo as 'The One') but not in genre-conventional form.

extra_patterns: Supernatural/ghost element creates a horror overlay
Historical trauma as collective rather than individual
corpus.notes: The rebirth archetype maps but the Atonement node is complex — Sethe's community reconciliation is more complex than straightforward atonement. Non-linear time IS present, suggesting the genre coverage may be higher than scored.

extra_patterns: Family dynasty structure creates a multi-generational arc
Michael's transformation from outsider to Don overlays the Transformation archetype
corpus.notes: Michael's rise-and-fall is unique because the 'desperate grip' phase happens across the sequels, not within the first film. The territory constraint is present but operates at family rather than street level.

extra_patterns: Revenge archetype overlay (Ahab's obsession)
Philosophical digressions on whaling have no graph counterpart
Narrator-observer (Ishmael) creates frame structure
corpus.notes: The quest structure maps but imperfectly. The objective is NOT won (the whale kills Ahab), and the cohesion test is inverted (the crew's cohesion dissolves). The revenge overlay is arguably the dominant archetype.

extra_patterns: Coming-of-age parallel arc (Chihiro matures)
Environmental/ecological themes
corpus.notes: Perfect voyage-and-return. The spirit world is the other world, and Chihiro's maturation (coming-of-age overlay) adds depth the archetype graph doesn't capture.

extra_patterns: Decade-spanning time structure creates episodic quality
corpus.notes: Textbook comedy in romantic comedy genre. The long time span is unusual but the structural nodes map precisely.

extra_patterns: Team-ensemble structure not captured by single-protagonist sacrifice graph
corpus.notes: The sacrifice archetype maps but the Resistance phase is brief — Harry accepts quickly. The action genre's physical rules are bent by Hollywood physics.

extra_patterns: Dual-investigation structure (Clarice investigates Buffalo Bill while Lecter investigates Clarice)
corpus.notes: Perfect mystery-thriller. The dual investigation adds a psychological layer — Lecter's counter-investigation creates tension the mystery graph doesn't fully capture.

extra_patterns: Ancestor-guided journey archetype (non-Western) strongly present
Music as thematic connector
corpus.notes: Perfect voyage-and-return to the Land of the Dead. The generational interaction constraint IS present (Miguel and Mama Coco) — this may be a scoring error.

extra_patterns: Political/dynastic structure adds a Rise and Fall overlay
Prophetic/messianic arc not captured by Hero's Journey graph
corpus.notes: The Hero's Journey maps through Part One and Two but the Return phases are incomplete — Paul doesn't return to an ordinary world. The prophetic/messianic dimension extends beyond the archetype.

extra_patterns: Watson as narrator-observer frame
corpus.notes: Definitive mystery-detective pairing. Holmes operates independently of institutions (soft miss). The Watson frame adds narrative distance.

extra_patterns: Buddy-comedy dynamic (Woody and Buzz) adds comedy archetype overlay
corpus.notes: Woody's journey into the wider world (Sid's house) and return to Andy maps perfectly to Voyage and Return.

extra_patterns: Hero's Journey overlay (general to slave to champion)
Historical/political dimension
corpus.notes: Perfect revenge mapping in action genre. The Hero's Journey overlay is clear — Maximus undergoes classic departure/initiation/return.

extra_patterns: Dystopian world-building provides the SF spine
Resistance/underground network adds political thriller overlay
corpus.notes: The escape archetype maps strongly through the setup but the novel's ambiguous ending means the escape attempt and freedom nodes are unresolved. The archetype anticipates resolution that the work deliberately withholds.

extra_patterns: Meta-commentary on horror conventions
Mystery-investigation overlay (who is the killer?)
corpus.notes: The monster archetype maps but the 'call to confront' is absent — Sidney doesn't choose to fight, she's forced to. The meta-commentary layer has no graph counterpart.

extra_patterns: Non-linear flashback structure tied to quiz show format
corpus.notes: Perfect rags-to-riches. The quiz-show structure provides a novel frame that the archetype graph doesn't capture.

extra_patterns: Existential/philosophical dimension (Chigurh as force of nature)
Sheriff's reflective frame narrative
corpus.notes: The revenge mapping is a stretch — Llewelyn doesn't vow revenge, he's simply trying to survive after taking the money. The film subverts archetype expectations by denying cathartic confrontation.

extra_patterns: Grief processing as parallel emotional arc
Rebirth overlay (Carl rediscovers life through Russell)
corpus.notes: Strong quest mapping. The objective technically shifts (reaching Paradise Falls → saving Kevin → accepting new family), so the 'Objective Won' node is transformed rather than straightforwardly achieved.

extra_patterns: Class commentary as thematic engine
Dual-family mirror structure
corpus.notes: The rise-and-fall maps but the Fall is sudden violence rather than a sustained decline. The class commentary and architectural metaphor (upstairs/downstairs) exceed the graph's structural capture.

extra_patterns: Frame story (grandfather reading to grandson)
Comedy overlay
Romance overlay
Self-aware/meta-narrative element
corpus.notes: Strong quest-adventure but deliberately compressed — the 'final approach' is almost nonexistent, going directly from trials to ordeal. The multi-genre overlay is the film's distinctive quality.

extra_patterns: Ensemble drama structure (12 characters with distinct arcs)
Institutional critique
corpus.notes: The mystery archetype maps partially — Juror #8 uncovers reasonable doubt through investigation. But the 'confrontation' is social (convincing other jurors) rather than the physical or revelatory confrontation the archetype expects.

extra_patterns: Gender identity/disguise as thematic engine
Coming-of-age parallel
corpus.notes: Strong Hero's Journey. The Road Back is compressed — Mulan goes directly from the Ordeal to a new Resurrection crisis (the Huns at the palace) without a true return journey.

extra_patterns: Protagonist switch (Marion to Sam/Lila) mid-film
Psychological portrait replaces investigation
corpus.notes: The mystery maps but unusually — the initial clues and breakthrough are weak because the audience discovers the truth through shock (the shower scene) rather than investigation. The protagonist switch is structurally unprecedented.

extra_patterns: Rebirth overlay (Schindler transforms from profiteer to savior)
Historical testimony dimension
corpus.notes: Strong sacrifice archetype but the 'World Saved' node is tempered — Schindler saves 1,100 lives while millions die. The resolution is partial, which is the film's devastating moral point.

extra_patterns: Coming-of-age parallel
Twist revelation that training was real warfare
corpus.notes: Strong Hero's Journey in SF. The 'Resurrection' is subverted — the final test IS the climactic battle, but Ender doesn't know it. The revelation that he committed genocide unknowingly inverts the expected triumphant return.

extra_patterns: The revenge quest itself is a trap set by the antagonist
Mystery overlay (why was I imprisoned?)
corpus.notes: Powerful revenge-thriller. The moral crossroads is subverted — by the time Oh Dae-su could reconsider, the trap has already sprung. The revenge-as-trap inversion extends beyond the archetype.

archetype_coverage_by_type.note: Highest coverage. The Hero's Journey is the most universally applicable archetype.
Very high coverage when correctly identified.
High coverage. Objective Won node sometimes subverted or transformed.
Perfect coverage across all sampled works. The simplest archetype structurally.
Preparation and World Restored are the most commonly missed nodes.
High coverage. Atonement node is the most complex to map.
Perfect coverage. Comedy's broad structure accommodates many specific implementations.
Limited sample but perfect mapping (To Kill a Mockingbird).
Pursuit and Moral Crossroads are most commonly missed or subverted.
Setbacks phase and Freedom node are most commonly compressed or withheld.
Resistance and World Saved nodes are most commonly compressed.
Lowest mean coverage. Initial Clues and Breakthrough are the most commonly weak or subverted nodes.
Limited sample but perfect mapping (Black Swan).
The Fall node is sometimes sudden rather than sustained.

systematic_gaps.description: The current graphs model single-protagonist arcs. Works with ensemble casts (Game of Thrones, Twelve Angry Men) or dual protagonists (Gone Girl) require overlaying multiple archetype instances.
Flashback structures, frame narratives, and unreliable narration are narrative techniques not captured by the linear graph model.
Works that comment on their own genre conventions (Scream, The Princess Bride) operate at a meta-level the graphs don't capture.
Some works deliberately subvert the expected resolution (The Handmaid's Tale, Gone Girl, No Country for Old Men). The graphs assume resolution; some works deny it.
Social commentary (Get Out), philosophical dimensions (The Matrix, Moby-Dick), and allegory operate as thematic layers that the structural graphs don't represent.

vocabulary_recommendations: Consider adding a 'Subverted' variant for Resolution nodes to capture deliberately withheld or inverted endings.
Consider a multi-protagonist extension that allows parallel archetype instances to be tracked simultaneously.
The Trickster role (from non-Western analysis) could enrich Western archetype graphs — several works feature trickster-like characters not captured by existing roles.
A 'Frame Narrative' structural element could capture works with narrator-observers (Nick in Gatsby, Watson in Holmes, the grandfather in Princess Bride).
